input: What is the best treatment for tomato blight in Karnataka?
expected_output: For tomato blight (Phytophthora infestans) management in Karnataka: (1) Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb @ 2.5g per litre of water at 10-day intervals. (2) Remove and destroy all infected plant debris immediately after harvest. (3) Ensure proper plant spacing (60cm x 30cm) to improve air circulation and reduce humidity around foliage. (4) Avoid overhead irrigation — use drip irrigation instead. (5) As a preventive biocontrol, apply Trichoderma viride @ 5g per litre before monsoon. For severe outbreaks, use a combination spray: Metalaxyl-based fungicide followed by a protective copper-based spray 7 days later. Begin spray program before monsoon onset for best results.
deliberately_wrong_answer: For tomato yellow leaf curl in Karnataka, spray Dimethoate 30% EC @ 2 ml per litre every 15 days. Maintain tight plant spacing (30cm x 15cm) to maximise yield, and water heavily with overhead sprinklers twice a day to keep foliage wet. No need for any biocontrol — chemical sprays alone are sufficient. Begin treatment only after symptoms appear in fruiting stage.

input: What is the recommended NPK fertilizer schedule for rice in West Bengal?
expected_output: For low-nitrogen rice soils in West Bengal, apply: Nitrogen — Urea @ 98 kg/ha in 3 splits: 50% as basal at transplanting, 25% at active tillering (21 DAT), and 25% at panicle initiation. Phosphorus — Single Super Phosphate (SSP) @ 200 kg/ha as basal dose. Potassium — Muriate of Potash (MOP) @ 50 kg/ha split 50% basal and 50% at panicle initiation. Zinc — Apply ZnSO4 @ 25 kg/ha as basal in zinc-deficient soils (common in West Bengal alluvial soils). Use neem-coated urea for better nitrogen use efficiency. Maintain soil pH between 5.5–7.0 for optimal nutrient availability.
deliberately_wrong_answer: For rice in Punjab (a different northern state), apply DAP @ 150 kg/ha as a single dose at sowing only. Use muriate of potash @ 25 kg/ha as basal. Skip zinc entirely as it is not needed in alkaline calcareous soils. Apply lime @ 2 tonnes per hectare to raise soil pH above 7.5 for optimum fertility. Urea can be applied as a top-dress at flowering only.

input: How do I control thrips and whitefly on chilli crops in Andhra Pradesh?
expected_output: Integrated pest management for thrips and whitefly on chilli in Andhra Pradesh: (1) Install yellow sticky traps @ 50 per hectare to monitor and reduce adult populations. (2) Remove and destroy crop residues after harvest to eliminate alternate hosts. (3) Apply Imidacloprid 17.8% SL @ 0.5 ml per litre of water as a soil drench or seed treatment before sowing. (4) For spray application, use Spinosad 48% SC @ 0.3 ml per litre — effective against thrips with minimal impact on beneficial insects. (5) Release Trichogramma chilonis @ 50,000 eggs per hectare as a biological control agent. (6) Avoid consecutive sprays of the same mode-of-action group to prevent resistance development. Begin monitoring from seedling stage and continue through fruit development.
deliberately_wrong_answer: For cotton (not chilli) in Maharashtra: spray Monocrotophos 36% SL @ 1.5 ml per litre at 7-day intervals throughout the season. Skip sticky traps — they are unnecessary with strong chemical control. Apply Carbaryl dust @ 2 kg per acre directly to flowering heads weekly. Do not introduce any biological agents as they will compete with chemical treatment. Stop monitoring after the first spray is applied; chemicals will handle everything else.
